mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates above 4.5% compress equity valuations through higher discount rates applied to future corporate cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window or positive returns dominate in over 65% of observations despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 3-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals imminent labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in over 70% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and housing demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month industrial production growth exceeding 0.5% confirms robust manufacturing output that outweighs monetary tightening headwinds, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth fails to generate positive 20-day returns during tightening periods, with mean return below 0.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding labor demand that supports near-term corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS momentum shows zero predictive power for 5-day returns, with Rank IC consistently near zero across all regimes.

mechanism_narrative: The co-occurrence of elevated consumer price inflation above 205 and federal funds rates above 4.5% creates a stagflationary risk premium that suppresses equity valuations, yielding negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint high-inflation and high-rate regime fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth decelerating below 1.0% signals a breaking economic momentum that triggers risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Sub-1.0% GDP growth fails to trigger negative 20-day returns during late-cycle phases, with mean return exceeding 0.1%.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production confirm a manufacturing contraction that triggers immediate sell-offs, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Consecutive industrial production declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns, with hit rate below 50%.
